### Post by jeshua.brach on 2009-02-01
Hello everybody,

Although I've set the refresh rate in NVIDIA X Server Settings to 
75 Hz and saved it to etc/X11/xorg.conf after login I get anyway
a (horrible) 60 Hz display !?

Here's my xorg.conf
TIA, hoping someone can help to resolve

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@vernadsky)  Thu Jun  5 09:26:53 UTC 2008


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

EndSection

### Post by Le-Dart on 2009-02-01
You may have done it this way already. But the best way is to open nVidia settings from a terminal, as root not a user;

sudo nvidia-settings

Then set your resolution, refresh rate and click on "Save to X-Configuration File" and "Save".

That did it for me.

Is the Horizontal and Vertical refresh setting correct for your monitor? Check in the handbook or on-line,

---
